name: Butterscotch Patties
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly butter three large cookie sheets.
2. Combine sugar, corn syrup, water, and milk in a medium-sized heavy saucepan. Bring to boiling over medium heat, stirring constantly.
3. Cook, stirring often, until the temperature reaches 260°F on a candy thermometer; add butter or margarine.
4. Continue cooking until the temperature reaches 280°F on a candy thermometer (a teaspoonful of syrup will separate into threads that are hard but not brittle when dropped in cold water). Remove from heat.
5. Drop hot syrup from the tip of a teaspoon onto the cookie sheets to form 1-inch patties, or pour into a 9x9x2-inch pan and mark into small squares when almost set.
6. When firm, turn out and break apart. Makes 8 dozen 1-inch patties.
